GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Ciufoli is the jewellery maison fusing Italian craft with Australian flair", ""A jewel must complement one's personality, make it shine, express its confidence," says Giacomo Ciufoli, the Australian-born jeweller behind his namesake Ciufoli maison. It's a fitting philosophy for the designer whose jewellery tells of a distinctly personal story. Born to a Russian mother and Italian father, Ciufoli's jewellery is informed by a childhood spent alternating between Italy and Australia. Time in each country, visiting cities like Florence and Rome while abroad, and learning the local Australian landscape, proved instrumental for the designer during his formative years. It meant Ciufoli was able to absorb the rich cultural and historical contexts afforded by each place, laying a foundation of inspiration he would later channel into a high-end jewellery house founded in 2017 with the launch of his first collection, Serpe. With a love of travel instilled in him from an early age, Ciufoli was originally interested in pursuing a career in aviation, but refocused his energies closer to home. Determined to learn about his own family's heritage in craft—Ciufoli is a third-generation artist—he returned to Valenza, in the Piedmont region of Italy, where each of his pieces are hand-made today. There, an emphasis on slow living and an attention to craft align with Ciufoli's own meticulous approach to jewellery, one that utilises tradition in balance with innovation, to produce modern creations women will wear for years to come and hopefully pass down. Every piece is designed in Australia first, before a team of 10 highly skilled artisans—with 40 years of expertise among them—sets out to realise Ciufoli's visions in the maison's Italian workshop. Timeless designs such as oval cut and cushion-cut diamonds, are available to view exclusively by private appointment, with opportunities to commission bespoke creations as well. These classic offerings sit alongside contemporary settings, such as Ciufoli's romantic heart-shaped diamond that resonates with women today, and together attest to the brand's strength in catering to different sensibilities of luxury. These take form in Ciufoli's highly anticipated Segreto collection, a unique bridal collection that launches in its newly-opened Adelaide boutique on August 4, with virtual private appointments also available for people across Australia and overseas until travel restrictions ease. Translating to 'secret' in Italian, Segreto features a collection of rings bound by an element only seen by its wearers: a hidden gem on the inside of the band. A beautiful ruby heart, intended to be seen only by the couple to which the ring belongs, is set beneath the crowning center stone in a romantic yet discreet gesture, solidifying their commitment to one another. It is a feat of craftsmanship mastered by few jewellers in the world, and though incredibly challenging to execute, appears effortless in Ciufoli's collection. This delicate feature can be found in each and every Ciufoli engagement ring, meaning there is a style for every discerning bride. Take, for example, the Perpetuo style, designed to evoke a sense of longevity, symbolised by three adjacent stones that subtly point to the past, present and future. A similar spirit of timelessness also manifests in the Iconico, which harks back to Hollywood's Golden Era and features stripped back bands that shift the spotlight onto the central diamond. A bride in search of a ring that feels reminiscent of another era, but is still beautiful today, may look to D-Iconico Pop, inspired by the decade of decadence, the 1920s, while yet another may be intrigued by Ballerini, which as the name suggests, takes its design cues from music and movement. At a moment where we are savouring precious objects and time spent with loved ones, Ciufoli's special creations hone in on the elements of jewellery we are now valuing most: the preservation of craft, unparalleled beauty and a spirit of sentimentality—belonging to the designer, and our own—that is palpable at the touch. Book your private appointment online here."
